meta.department_name: Department of Computer Science and Engineering

questions.question: How many faculty members are there in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at IIT Jammu?
questions.expected_answer: There are 15 faculty members in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at IIT Jammu.

questions.question: Which faculty member is supervising the research of Abhilash Kumar Das, who is working on Cryptology?
evidence: Abhilash Kumar Das | Research: Cryptology | Supervisors: Sumit Kumar Pandey
Sumit Kumar Pandey | Assistant Professor | Research: Cryptology

questions.question: Which two research areas have the most connected faculty/students in the department?
questions.expected_answer: Image Processing, Computer Vision and Network and Cyber Security are the top two research areas with the most connected faculty/students.
evidence: Image Processing, Computer Vision (8 linked faculty/students)
Network and Cyber Security (4 linked faculty/students)

questions.question: What are the key research areas that the faculty members of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at IIT Jammu are actively involved in?
questions.expected_answer: The key research areas include Cyber and Information Security, AI and Machine Learning, Software Engineering, Image Processing, Computer Vision, Network and Cyber Security, Deep Learning, Machine Leaning and Cyber Security, Parallel and Distributed Algorithms, Submodular Functions, Algorithmic Optimization, Parameterized Complexity, User Profiling, 5G Communication & Network, Adversarial Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision, Biometrics, Biometrics and Computer Vision, Computer Vision, Cryptology, Cyber Security, Database Indexing and Machine learning, Image Processing & Deep Learning, Network Security, NeuroSymbolic AI and Deep Learning, Secure Machine Learning.
evidence: The department has a good mix of young and experienced faculties in the domain of Cyber and Information Security, AI and Machine Learning, Software Engineering to name a few.

Some of the focus areas of the department include Image Processing, Computer Vision, Network and Cyber Security, Deep Learning, Machine Leaning and Cyber Security, Parallel and Distributed Algorithms, Submodular Functions, Algorithmic Optimization, Parameterized Complexity, User Profiling, 5G Communication & Network, Adversarial Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision, Biometrics, Biometrics and Computer Vision, Computer Vision, Cryptology, Cyber Security, Database Indexing and Machine learning, Image Processing & Deep Learning, Network Security, NeuroSymbolic AI and Deep Learning, Secure Machine Learning.
The department runs a regular B.Tech programme and M.Tech programmes in Information Security and Data Science.
